grafana/grafana#81765.body:
**Why is this needed**:

In November 2023, [Elastic announced](https://www.elastic.co/blog/esql-elasticsearch-piped-query-language) a new query language, ES|QL (Elasticsearch Query Language).

I acknowledge that, currently, ES|QL is still in technical preview. From the [Elasticsearch 8.12 docs](https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/8.12/esql.html):

> Do not use ES|QL on production environments. This functionality is in technical preview and may be changed or removed in a future release.

Given that [Grafana Labs partner with Elastic to build the Elasticsearch plugin for Grafana](https://grafana.com/blog/2021/03/04/why-were-partnering-with-elastic-to-build-the-elasticsearch-plugin-for-grafana/), I imagine this is probably already on an internal to-do list. In submitting this feature request, I'm wondering if I can "kill two birds with one stone":

- Find out when Elastic plan to promote ES|QL from "technical preview" to "generally available" (GA)
- Find out when this feature might be added to the query editor (I'm guessing, not until ES|QL is GA)

**What would you like to be added**:

Support for entering ES|QL queries directly into the Elasticsearch query editor in Grafana.

**Who is this feature for?**

Grafana users who use Elasticsearch as a data source.
grafana/grafana#81765.title: Elasticsearch query editor: add support for entering queries using ES|QL syntax

grafana/grafana#86860.body:
**Why is this needed**:

I had noticed the grafana load times getting gradually longer over the past few months as I upgraded versions. [This issue](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/21074) helped reduce load times (on first access, i.e. login page) from 40s, down to 9s just by setting `gzip_enabled=true`. [The doc](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/setup-grafana/configure-grafana/#enable-gzip) mentions it defaulting to false for compatibility reasons, but true is the recommended value for most customers. With major version 11 of Grafana coming up, I'm wondering if now would be an opportune time to push a breaking change for the few that benefits the majority, and document it with release/upgrade notes, with instructions on identifying when gzip_enabled should be false.

As a Grafana user of 6 years, this was the first time I had come across the need for this config, and believe it could benefit a lot of users, especially as more features get added to this excellent tool that add additional network calls in the browser.


**What would you like to be added**:

1. In `grafana.ini`'s `[server]` section, replace `;enable_gzip = false` to `enable_gzip = true`
2. Add documentation in release/upgrade notes for this change, and what situations/logs that would indicate `enable_gzip` should be disabled


**Who is this feature for?**

All users
grafana/grafana#86860.title: Enable gzip compression by default

grafana/grafana#99450.body:
<!-- Please only use this template for submitting feature requests -->

**Why is this needed**:

Currently, when using the Group to Nested Tables transformation, it's not possible to apply overrides to the columns that are part of the nested structure. This limitation causes inconsistencies in the table formatting, especially when the nested columns lose their overrides (e.g., custom colors, alignments, or value mappings) previously applied in a flat structure.


**What would you like to be added**:

Enable Overrides to be applied to nested columns within the Group to Nested Tables transformation. This feature would allow users to:

- Apply column-specific styles (colors, value mappings, etc.) to nested fields.
- Retain formatting from the flat table even after grouping.
- Improve usability and visual consistency for complex datasets with hierarchical structures.

**Who is this feature for?**
This feature is for users who use nested tables to group data but also require the ability to format individual fields for clarity.
grafana/grafana#99450.title: Allow Overrides for Nested Table Columns in Grouped Transformations

grafana/grafana#116545.body:
Add a single-evaluator mode for [Grafana Alerting High Availability](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/v12.3/alerting/set-up/configure-high-availability/) deployments where only one instance evaluates alert rules, reducing query load on downstream data sources.

Relates to https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/61512.

#### Problem

When running Grafana in HA mode, currently all instances evaluate all alert rules independently. This multiplies the query load on data sources by the number of Grafana instances. For deployments with many alert rules, this results in increased resource usage on data sources, and unnecessary duplicate work.

#### Solution

In single-evaluator mode, only one instance (position 0 in the HA cluster of Grafanas) evaluates all alert rules. Other instances skip evaluation entirely but remain fully functional for API requests and Alertmanager operations. There's no load distribution across instances. 

When the primary instance fails, failover happens automatically when Memberlist detects the failure and node positions are reassigned automatically. The new position 0 starts evaluating rules. A short evaluation gap might occur during failover.

#### Configuration

This mode has to be enabled explicitly in the config:

```ini                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        
  [unified_alerting]                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            
  ha_single_node_evaluation = true                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
```
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
Like [standard HA mode](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/v12.3/alerting/set-up/configure-high-availability/), it also requires clustering to be configured (Memberlist or Redis).

#### Notes

Potentially, `ha_single_node_evaluation` could be replaced with a setting that specifies the number of nodes that evaluate rules, increasing reliability. For example, when set to 2 nodes with positions 0 and 1 could evaluate rules.
grafana/grafana#116545.title: Alerting: Add single-node evaluation option for HA mode

grafana/grafana#116883.body:
**User Experience:**


Users relying on assistive technologies, such as screen readers, cannot identify the purpose of the 'Post/Get' radio buttons due to the absence of an accessible group label. 


**Steps to reproduce**:
1. Create a New Dashboard.  
1. Navigate to "Add visualization" button and hit ENTER key. "Select data source" popup will appear.  
1. Navigate to the TestData list item select it.   
1. Now navigate to all the dialogs which is appears after invoking the add buttons like add link , add action , add value mappings , etc. under Visualization pane. 
1. Observe that the screen reader does not announce the group label for the 'Post/Get' radio buttons under the "Add action" section. 


**Actual Result**:
Screen readers fail to announce the group label for the 'Post/Get' radio buttons, leaving users unable to understand the purpose of these options. 
Observation: 
The 'Post/Get' radio buttons are missing a programmatically associated group label, causing screen readers to announce the buttons individually without context. 

_Issue1_: Same issue reproduces for the checkboxes controls under 'Selection options' heading by invoking 'Add variable' button under Variables tab in the settings. 
Navigation: Select “ Add visualization” button > Select testdata list item> Select Back to dashboard in the tool bar > Select settings button and invoke it > Go to the Variables tab under settings> Invoke Add variable button> Verify that the screen reader announces the group label for checkboxes controls  under the “Selection options “ heading on the “Add Variable “ tab. 

_Issue2_: Same issue reproduces for the radio buttons under 'General' heading by invoking 'Add variable' button under Variables tab in the settings. 
Navigation: Select “ Add visualization” button > Select testdata list item> Select Back to dashboard in the tool bar > Select settings button and invoke it > Go to the Variables tab under settings> Invoke Add variable button> Verify that the screen reader announces the ‘hide’  group label for radio buttons  under the “General “ heading on the “Add Variable “ tab. 
  

**Expected Result**
The 'Post/Get' radio buttons should have a group label programmatically defined using semantic HTML elements such as <fieldset> and <legend> or ARIA attributes like aria-labelledby to ensure that screen readers announce the label as "Method." This will help users understand the purpose of the radio buttons.

grafana/grafana#117826.body:
## Description

The navigation dialog and the Share dialog do not have programmatically associated titles. When a screen reader user opens these dialogs, the screen reader announces an irrelevant or browser-generated title (e.g., "Example Dashboard - Dashboards - Grafana - Google Chrome dialog") instead of a meaningful, descriptive title.

This affects all dialog instances that lack a proper `aria-labelledby` or `aria-label` attribute.

**Affected areas:**
- Left navigation sidebar dialog
- Share dialog (opened via the "Share" button)

## Steps to reproduce

1. Open Grafana in Chrome on Windows
2. Enable JAWS screen reader
3. Use Tab to trigger and open the navigation dialog (or activate the "Share" button to open the Share dialog)
4. Listen to the screen reader announcement for the dialog title

## Actual behavior

The screen reader announces an irrelevant title (e.g., the browser window title appended with "dialog") instead of a meaningful and descriptive dialog title.

## Expected behavior

Each dialog should have a descriptive title that is programmatically associated with the dialog and announced by screen readers (e.g., "Navigation Options" for the nav dialog, "Share Dashboard" for the share dialog).

## Suggested fix

Associate a descriptive title with each dialog using `aria-labelledby` pointing to a visible heading inside the dialog, or use `aria-label` to provide a concise title.

## WCAG criteria

- **4.1.2 Name, Role, Value** (Level A)
- Reference: [WAI-ARIA Dialog Pattern](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/dialog/)

## Possible duplicates in grafana/grafana

No exact duplicates found. Partially related:
- [#112678](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/112678) — Drawer: Duplicate role="dialog" attributes in Drawer (closed; addresses dialog role issues but not missing titles)

## Issue reference

Reference issue 02
grafana/grafana#117826.title: A11y: Navigation and Share dialogs are missing programmatic titles for screen readers

grafana/grafana#117827.body:
## Description

The "Bookmark" button and similar toggle buttons do not announce their pressed or unpressed state to screen readers when toggled. Screen reader users cannot determine whether the button is currently active or inactive.

**Affected areas:**
- "Bookmark" button in the navigation dialog
- "Favorite Star" button
- Toggle buttons below graphs (e.g., switching between `cpu_steal_pct` and `cpu_wait_pct`)

## Steps to reproduce

1. Open Grafana in Chrome on Windows
2. Enable JAWS screen reader
3. Navigate to the navigation dialog containing the "Bookmark" button
4. Use Tab to reach the "Bookmark" button
5. Listen to the screen reader announcement — note it does not announce the current state
6. Press Enter or Space to toggle the button's state
7. Observe that the screen reader does not announce the updated state

## Actual behavior

The "Bookmark" button does not provide any announcement for its state (pressed or unpressed) when focused or toggled.

## Expected behavior

The button should dynamically announce its state (e.g., "pressed" or "not pressed") to screen readers whenever the state changes.

## Suggested fix

Add the `aria-pressed` attribute to toggle buttons and dynamically update its value to `true` or `false` based on the button's current state.

```html
<button aria-label="Add to Bookmarks" aria-pressed="false" ...>
```

## WCAG criteria

- **4.1.2 Name, Role, Value** (Level A)

## Possible duplicates in grafana/grafana

- [#87801](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/87801) — Incorrect name & role is defined for 'Formatted data' & 'Download for excel' toggle buttons (closed; same pattern of toggle buttons missing proper state/role)

## Issue reference

Reference issue 03
grafana/grafana#117827.title: A11y: Toggle buttons (Bookmark, Favorite Star) do not announce pressed/unpressed state

grafana/grafana#117828.body:
## Description

Several expand/collapse buttons update their `aria-label` to reflect the current state, but the updated state is not announced by screen readers upon activation. The screen reader does not inform users whether the section is now expanded or collapsed.

**Affected areas:**
- "Section Bookmark" arrow icon button
- "Toggle Top Search Bar" button
- "Help" and "Profile" buttons
- "Open" and "Close Menu" buttons
- Expand/collapse row buttons in data tables

## Steps to reproduce

1. Open Grafana in Chrome on Windows
2. Enable JAWS screen reader
3. Navigate to a section containing an expand/collapse button (e.g., the "Section Bookmark" arrow icon)
4. Use Tab to reach the button
5. Press Enter or Space to toggle its state
6. Observe that the screen reader does not announce the updated state (e.g., "expanded" or "collapsed")

## Actual behavior

The button's `aria-label` updates to reflect the state (e.g., "Collapse section Bookmarks"), but the screen reader does not announce the updated expand/collapse state after activation.

## Expected behavior

The button should dynamically announce its updated expand/collapse state every time it is activated.

## Suggested fix

Use `aria-expanded` on the button element to programmatically communicate the expanded/collapsed state, rather than relying solely on `aria-label` changes:

```html
<button aria-expanded="true" aria-label="Section Bookmarks" ...>
```

Reference: [WAI-ARIA Disclosure Pattern](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/disclosure/)

## WCAG criteria

- **4.1.2 Name, Role, Value** (Level A)

## Possible duplicates in grafana/grafana

- [#86649](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/86649) — Change time settings: No state property is defined for the 'Change time settings' button (closed; same pattern — button doesn't announce expanded/collapsed state)
- [#66350](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/66350) — Aria label is not defined for "expand/collapse" button present for "Data source: AWS IoT Site wise" (open; expand/collapse button missing accessible name)
- [#53924](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/53924) — A11y: Implement aria-expanded for query rows (closed; same aria-expanded pattern)

## Issue reference

Reference issue 04
grafana/grafana#117828.title: A11y: Expand/collapse buttons do not announce their expanded or collapsed state

grafana/grafana#117832.body:
## Description

When navigating options in the Help dropdown menu, the screen reader announces every option's position as "1 of 1," regardless of how many options exist in the dropdown. This makes it impossible for screen reader users to know how many options are available or where they are within the list.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Open Grafana in Chrome on Windows
2. Enable JAWS screen reader
3. Navigate to the Help dropdown and activate it
4. Use arrow keys to navigate to the "Keyboard Shortcuts" option (or any other option)
5. Listen to the screen reader announcement for the position

## Actual behavior

The screen reader announces "1 of 1" for every option, regardless of the actual number of options in the dropdown.

## Expected behavior

The screen reader should announce the correct position of each option (e.g., "Keyboard Shortcuts, 3 of 5") based on its actual position within the dropdown.

## Suggested fix

Ensure the dropdown options are implemented using semantic HTML elements (e.g., `<ul>` and `<li>` for lists) or ARIA roles (`role="menu"` and `role="menuitem"`). If using custom markup, verify that `aria-setsize` and `aria-posinset` are correctly set, or let the browser compute them from proper list/menu structure.

## WCAG criteria

- **4.1.2 Name, Role, Value** (Level A)

## Possible duplicates in grafana/grafana

- [#89402](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/89402) — Tabs: Screen reader only announces 1 of 1 (closed; same issue — screen reader announces incorrect position "1 of 1" regardless of actual count)

## Issue reference

Reference issue 09
grafana/grafana#117832.title: A11y: Help dropdown options announce incorrect position (e.g., "1 of 1" regardless of actual count)

grafana/grafana#117835.body:
## Description

After closing the "Share" dialog, focus does not consistently return to the triggering "Share" button. It works correctly the first time, but on subsequent open/close cycles the focus fails to return, leaving keyboard and screen reader users disoriented.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Open Grafana in Chrome on Windows
2. Enable JAWS screen reader
3. Navigate to the "Share" button and activate it to open the Share dialog
4. Close the dialog using the close button or cancel button
5. Observe that focus returns to the "Share" button (works the first time)
6. Repeat: open and close the "Share" dialog again
7. Observe that focus does **not** return to the "Share" button on subsequent attempts

## Actual behavior

Focus returns to the "Share" button the first time the dialog is closed, but fails to do so on subsequent open/close cycles.

## Expected behavior

Focus should consistently return to the original triggering element (the "Share" button) every time the dialog is closed.

## Suggested fix

Ensure the dialog's close handler always restores focus to the element that triggered the dialog. Store a reference to the trigger element when the dialog opens, and call `.focus()` on it when the dialog closes.

Reference: [WAI-ARIA Dialog Pattern](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/dialog/)

## WCAG criteria

- **2.4.3 Focus Order** (Level A)

## Possible duplicates in grafana/grafana

- [#72807](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/72807) — Focus order does not return to "Set color" button after pressing esc key (closed; same pattern — focus not returned to triggering element after closing a dialog)
- [#95721](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/95721) — Dashboard: Keyboard focus is reset when deleting a panel (open; related focus management issue)

## Issue reference

Reference issue 12
grafana/grafana#117835.title: A11y: Focus does not consistently return to the "Share" button after closing the Share dialog

grafana/grafana#117836.body:
## Description

The heading hierarchy on several pages is incorrect. Heading levels are skipped (e.g., `<h1>` followed directly by `<h3>`, or `<h2>` followed by `<h6>`), which makes it difficult for screen reader users to understand the structure and relationships of the content.

**Affected areas:**
- **Profile page:** An `<h3>` heading appears directly after an `<h1>` heading, skipping `<h2>`. Additionally, the "Preferences" section has no heading at all.
- **Home page:** An `<h6>` heading appears directly after `<h2>`, and `<h1>` is missing entirely.
- Other pages have similar issues.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Open Grafana in Chrome on Windows
2. Enable JAWS screen reader
3. Navigate to the Profile page
4. Use the screen reader's heading navigation (e.g., press H in JAWS) to navigate through headings
5. Observe that the heading hierarchy skips from `<h1>` to `<h3>`, bypassing `<h2>`

## Actual behavior

Heading levels are skipped across multiple pages, and some sections lack headings entirely.

## Expected behavior

Headings should follow a logical, sequential order (e.g., `<h1>` followed by `<h2>`, then `<h3>`, etc.) to maintain a proper content hierarchy. All major sections should have appropriate headings.

## Suggested fix

Audit heading structure across all pages and update them to follow a sequential hierarchy. For example, on the Profile page, replace the `<h3>` with `<h2>` if it is a direct subsection of the `<h1>`, and add an `<h2>` for the "Preferences" section.

## WCAG criteria

- **1.3.1 Info and Relationships** (Level A)

## Possible duplicates in grafana/grafana

- [#73693](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/73693) — [Epic] Heading consistency across Grafana (incorrect hierarchy, additional headings required, incorrect headings) (open; tracks heading hierarchy issues across the application)
- [#106841](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/106841) — A11y: Audit H1 usage across Grafana (open; auditing heading usage)
- [#66560](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/66560) — Heading hierarchy is not defined sequentially in the "Explore" Screen (open; same issue in Explore)
- [#66554](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/66554) — Heading hierarchy is not defined sequentially in the "Time series graphs / Edit Panel" Screen (open; same issue in Edit Panel)

## Issue reference

Reference issue 13
grafana/grafana#117836.title: A11y: Heading hierarchy skips levels across multiple pages

grafana/grafana#117837.body:
## Description

Several form controls across Grafana lack programmatically associated labels. Screen reader users cannot determine the purpose of these controls when they receive focus.

**Affected instances:**
1. The dismiss notification checkbox on the Notification History page has no label.
2. The "Copy" and "Paste Time Range" buttons inside the Absolute Time Range panel are missing labels.
3. The "Type to Search (Country, City, Abbreviation)" field inside the Browser Time section has no label.
4. The "Server List" edit field and the "Hostname" combo box are not announced with their visual labels.
5. The expand/collapse row button is not announced with its visual label.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Open Grafana in Chrome on Windows
2. Enable JAWS screen reader
3. Navigate to the Notification History page
4. Use Tab to navigate to the dismiss notification checkbox
5. Listen to the screen reader announcement — observe that no label or purpose is announced

## Actual behavior

The checkbox (and other affected controls) are announced without a label, leaving screen reader users unable to understand their purpose.

## Expected behavior

Each form control should have a descriptive label that clearly conveys its purpose (e.g., "Dismiss Notification" for the checkbox).

## Suggested fix

Associate labels with form controls using `<label>` elements with a `for` attribute, or use `aria-label` / `aria-labelledby`:

```html
<label>
  <input type="checkbox" />
  Dismiss notification
</label>
```

Reference: [WAI Tutorials: Form Labels](https://www.w3.org/WAI/tutorials/forms/labels/)

## WCAG criteria

- **4.1.2 Name, Role, Value** (Level A)

## Possible duplicates in grafana/grafana

- [#108096](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/108096) — A11y: Edit panel: Some labels and form elements are not linked (closed; same pattern — labels not associated with form controls)
- [#79517](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/79517) — VariableInput fields do not have a programmatic name associated with them (closed; same pattern)
- [#106184](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/106184) — WCAG 4.1.2: Ensure Buttons have discernable text (closed; related — buttons missing accessible names)

## Issue reference

Reference issue 14

grafana/grafana#117846.body:
## Description

When the Grafana application is zoomed to 400% using the browser's zoom feature, some content is cut off, hidden, or otherwise inaccessible. Users with low vision who rely on high zoom levels cannot view all information on the page.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Open Grafana in Chrome on Windows
2. Use the browser's zoom feature (Ctrl + Plus or Ctrl + Mouse Wheel Up) to increase the page zoom to 400%
3. Review the page content and layout at 400% zoom
4. Observe areas where content is cut off, hidden, or not fully visible

## Actual behavior

At 400% zoom, some content is cut off, hidden, or inaccessible, preventing users from viewing all information.

## Expected behavior

All content should remain visible, accessible, and usable when the page is zoomed up to 400%, in accordance with WCAG 1.4.10 Reflow. Content should reflow into a single column without requiring horizontal scrolling (at a viewport width equivalent of 320 CSS pixels).

## Suggested fix

Update the page layout and CSS to ensure responsive design and proper reflow at high zoom levels. Key approaches:
- Use relative units (`rem`, `em`, `%`) instead of fixed pixel values
- Ensure containers use `overflow: auto` or `overflow: visible` rather than `overflow: hidden` where content may be clipped
- Test layouts at 320px equivalent viewport width

## WCAG criteria

- **1.4.10 Reflow** (Level AA)

## Possible duplicates in grafana/grafana

- [#82169](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/82169) — A11y: Content is not perceivable and/or accessible without requiring scrolling in two dimensions at 400% zoom (closed; exact duplicate — WCAG 1.4.10 Reflow at 400% zoom)
- [#113389](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/113389) — At 400% zoom mode, the controls present inside the 'Inspect' and 'More' controls are getting truncated (open; same 400% zoom truncation issue in specific area)
- [#107127](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/107127) — WCAG 1.4.4 Resize Text - Support text Resize up to 200 percent without loss of content or functionality (closed; related zoom/reflow issue)

## Issue reference

Reference issue 29

grafana/grafana#118639.body: ## Description The "Skip to Main Content" link allows users to bypass navigation only on the first attempt, failing to work on subsequent activations. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Open the Grafana dashboard application. 2. Enable the JAWS screen reader. 3. Use the **Tab** key to navigate to the "Skip to Main Content" link. 4. Press **Enter** to activate the link — observe that focus moves to the main content area. 5. Navigate back to the top of the page using the keyboard (e.g., **Shift + Tab**). 6. Use the **Tab** key again to navigate to the "Skip to Main Content" link. 7. Press **Enter** to activate the link a second time. 8. Observe that the link **does not work** on subsequent attempts — focus does not move to the main content area. ## Actual Behavior The "Skip to Main Content" link allows users to bypass navigation only on the first attempt, failing to work on subsequent activations. ## Expected Behavior The "Skip to Main Content" link should work every time it is activated, consistently moving the focus to the main content area. ## User Impact If the "Skip to Main Content" link does not work consistently, keyboard and screen reader users may be forced to navigate through repetitive content multiple times. ## Element Source ```html Skip to main content ``` ## Suggested Fix Ensure the skip link target (`#pageContent`) correctly receives and retains focus on every activation. This may require setting `tabindex="-1"` on the target element and programmatically managing focus via JavaScript so that repeated activations reliably move focus to the main content area. **Reference:** [WCAG Technique G1 — Skip Navigation](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Techniques/general/G1) | Field | Value | |---|---| | **Reporter ref #** | 1 | | **WCAG Criterion** | 2.4.1 Bypass Blocks (Level A) | | **Severity** | Sev3 | | **Category** | No Bypass Blocker | | **Page** | Generic (all pages) | ## Related Issues in grafana/grafana | Issue | Title | State | Relevance | |---|---|---|---| | [#60816](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/60816) | A11y(Nav) 251858: When navigating with the keyboard there is no way to skip the nav straight to the content | Closed | **Near-duplicate** — same WCAG criterion, requested skip-to-content link (which was then added) | | [#82188](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/82188) | A11y: no direct access to the primary content of the Web page | Closed | **Related** — 2.4.1 Bypass Blocks, focus taking 15 tabs to reach content | | [#77263](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/77263) | Navigation: Improve MegaMenu a11y | Closed | **Related** — mentions Skip to Main Content link as part of nav a11y | | [#79521](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/79521) | [accessibility issue] Landmarks are missing and incorrectly marked up | Closed | **Related** — notes `main` element anchored incorrectly, which is the skip link target | Searched: "[skip to main content](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues?q=skip+to+main+content)", "[skip to content label:type/accessibility](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues?q=skip+to+content+label%3Atype%2Faccessibility)", "[bypass blocks](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues?q=bypass+blocks)", "[label:wcag/2.4.1](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues?q=label%3Awcag%2F2.4.1)", "[skip link label:type/accessibility](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues?q=skip+link+label%3Atype%2Faccessibility)". **Assessment:** This issue is **distinct** from the above. Prior issues resulted in the skip link being added and landmarks being fixed. This bug is that the skip link exists but fails on subsequent activations — a behavioral regression not covered by the closed issues. ## Test Environment - **Browser:** Google Chrome - **OS:** Windows 11 - **Assistive Technology:** JAWS screen reader - **Input:** Keyboard navigation

## Description The status message "Press ESC to exit kiosk mode" is not announced by the screen reader after activating the kiosk mode button, leaving screen reader users unaware of how to exit kiosk mode. **Note:** This issue exists for all similar instances. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Open the Grafana dashboard application. 2. Enable the JAWS screen reader. 3. Use the **Tab** key to navigate to the kiosk mode button. 4. Press **Enter** or **Space** to activate kiosk mode. 5. Observe that the status message "Press ESC to exit kiosk mode" is displayed visually. 6. Listen to the screen reader — the status message is **not** announced. ## Actual Behavior The status message "Press ESC to exit kiosk mode" is not announced by the screen reader after activating the kiosk mode button, leaving screen reader users unaware of how to exit kiosk mode. ## Expected Behavior The status message "Press ESC to exit kiosk mode" should be announced by the screen reader immediately after activating the kiosk mode button. ## User Impact Without the status message being announced, screen reader users may not know how to exit kiosk mode. ## Element Source N/A — the status message element does not use an ARIA live region. ## Suggested Fix Wrap the "Press ESC to exit kiosk mode" message in an ARIA live region so it is announced by assistive technologies when it appears. For example: ```html
Press ESC to exit kiosk mode
``` Alternatively, use `aria-live="assertive"` if the message should interrupt the current screen reader output. **Reference:** [ARIA22: Using role=status to present status messages](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/Techniques/aria/ARIA22) | Field | Value | |---|---| | **Reporter ref #** | 7 | | **WCAG Criterion** | 4.1.3 Status Messages (Level AA) | | **Severity** | Sev3 | | **Category** | Status Message | | **Page** | Generic (all pages with kiosk mode) | ## Related Issues in grafana/grafana | Issue | Title | State | Relevance | |---|---|---|---| | [#86659](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/86659) | Dashboard: Screen reader is not narrating 'No results found for your query' information for 'Search for dashboards & folders' edit | Closed | **Related** — same WCAG criterion (4.1.3), same pattern of a status message not announced by screen reader, different component (dashboard search vs kiosk mode) | Searched: "[kiosk mode screen reader](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues?q=kiosk+mode+screen+reader)", "[kiosk mode accessibility](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues?q=kiosk+mode+accessibility)", "[kiosk mode escape](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues?q=kiosk+mode+escape)", "[aria-live kiosk](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues?q=aria-live+kiosk)", "[status messages 4.1.3](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues?q=status+messages+4.1.3)", "[kiosk mode announce](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues?q=kiosk+mode+announce)", "[kiosk label:type/accessibility](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues?q=kiosk+label%3Atype%2Faccessibility)", "[label:wcag/4.1.3](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues?q=label%3Awcag%2F4.1.3)". **Assessment:** This is a **novel issue**. The closed #86659 shares the same WCAG criterion and failure pattern but targets a different component. No existing issue covers kiosk mode status message accessibility. ## Test Environment - **Browser:** Google Chrome - **OS:** Windows 11 - **Assistive Technology:** JAWS screen reader - **Input:** Keyboard navigation

grafana/grafana#118643.body:
## Description

The focus indicator is missing for location elements inside the table on the dashboards page.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open the Grafana dashboard application.
2. Navigate to the dashboards page containing the table with location elements.
3. Use the **Tab** key to navigate through the interactive elements in the table.
4. Observe when the focus reaches a location element.
5. Note that **no visible focus indicator** appears on the location element, making it unclear that it is focused.

## Actual Behavior

The focus indicator is missing for location elements inside the table on the dashboards page.

## Expected Behavior

Location elements inside the table should have a clear and visible focus indicator to ensure keyboard users can identify when the element is focused.

## User Impact

Without a focus indicator, keyboard users cannot determine when a location element is focused.

## Element Source

N/A — the location elements lack CSS focus styles.

## Suggested Fix

Add visible focus styles to the location elements within the dashboards table. For example:

```css
/* Ensure location elements in the dashboards table have a visible focus ring */
.dashboard-table .location-element:focus,
.dashboard-table .location-element:focus-visible {
  outline: 2px solid #4C7CF0;
  outline-offset: 2px;
}
```

Ensure focus styles meet WCAG 2.2 minimum contrast requirements (at least 3:1 against adjacent colors) and are not removed by `outline: none` resets.

**Reference:** [WCAG Understanding 2.4.7 — Focus Visible](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/focus-visible.html)

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Reporter ref #** | 25 |
| **WCAG Criterion** | 2.4.7 Focus Visible (Level AA) |
| **Severity** | Sev3 |
| **Category** | Focus Indicator |
| **Page** | Dashboard |

## Related Issues in grafana/grafana

| Issue | Title | State | Relevance |
|---|---|---|---|
| [#116512](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/116512) | [SEV2] Focus indicator not visible on Data source list items | Open | **Related** — same WCAG criterion, different component (data source list) |
| [#116516](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/116516) | [SEV2] Focus not visible on Queries, Graph, Table in Outline | Open | **Related** — same WCAG criterion, different component (Explore pane) |
| [#114227](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/114227) | PieChart: Focus indicator not visible on datalink | Open | **Related** — same criterion, panel visualization context |
| [#117286](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/117286) | [Sev2] Luminosity ratio of focused list items < 3:1 in combo boxes | Open | **Related** — focus visibility contrast issue |
| [#116515](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/116515) | [SEV2] Keyboard focus not visible on URL field under "Add Link" tab (dark mode) | Closed | **Related** — same criterion, dashboard context; recently fixed |

Searched: "[focus visible dashboard label:type/accessibility](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues?q=focus+visible+dashboard+label%3Atype%2Faccessibility)", "[focus indicator table](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues?q=focus+indicator+table)", "[focus indicator location](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues?q=focus+indicator+location)", "[focus visible dashboards table](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues?q=focus+visible+dashboards+table)", "[label:wcag/2.4.7](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues?q=label%3Awcag%2F2.4.7)", "[focus location dashboard label:type/accessibility](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues?q=focus+location+dashboard+label%3Atype%2Faccessibility)".

**Assessment:** This issue is **distinct** (specific to location elements in the dashboards table) but part of a broader pattern of missing focus indicators across the application. The related open issues above affect different components. Cross-referencing is recommended when filing.

## Test Environment

- **Browser:** Google Chrome
- **OS:** Windows 11
- **Assistive Technology:** JAWS screen reader
- **Input:** Keyboard navigation

grafana/grafana#118836.body:
### What happened?

I'm using the latest `grafana/grafana` Docker image.

```
msg="Starting Grafana" version=12.4.0 commit=d1729c53a7f44e2e58947eb44eb896c2fb1c30b3 branch=release-12.4.0 compiled=2026-02-24T09:41:41-05:00
```

After a recent upgrade to the latest tag, startup is failing when trying to do a database migration.

The failing migration is for `alert_rule.rule_group`:

```
logger=migrator t=2026-02-24T09:41:41.8974314-05:00 level=info msg="Starting DB migrations"
logger=migrator t=2026-02-24T09:41:41.909568971-05:00 level=info msg="Executing migration" id="ensure rule_group column is case sensitive in returned results"
logger=migrator t=2026-02-24T09:41:41.910216836-05:00 level=error msg="Executing migration failed" id="ensure rule_group column is case sensitive in returned results" error="Error 1273 (HY000): Unknown collation: 'utf8mb4_0900_as_cs'" duration=643.499µs
logger=migrator t=2026-02-24T09:41:41.910250481-05:00 level=error msg="Exec failed" error="Error 1273 (HY000): Unknown collation: 'utf8mb4_0900_as_cs'" sql="ALTER TABLE alert_rule MODIFY rule_group VARCHAR(190) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_0900_as_cs NOT NULL;"
logger=migrator t=2026-02-24T09:41:41.911185872-05:00 level=info msg="Unlocking database"
Error: ✗ migration failed (id = ensure rule_group column is case sensitive in returned results): Error 1273 (HY000): Unknown collation: 'utf8mb4_0900_as_cs'
```

I'm using MariaDB 11 (`11.4.2-MariaDB-ubu2404`) as a back-end database for Grafana.

The migration comes from this PR:

https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/111336/changes

The migration tries to force `alert_rule.rule_group` to `utf8mb4_0900_as_cs` collation.

Unfortuantely, MariaDB does not support any of the Unicode 9 collations (`utf8mb4_0900_*`) and only Unicode 14 instead (`utf8mb4_1400_*`).

I do not see a way of working around this migration (e.g. by changing the collation to another case-sensitive `utf8mb4_1400_*` instead) as the migration hard-codes `utf8mb4_0900_as_cs`.

I have rolled back to a pinned `grafana/grafana:12.3` docker image.

### What did you expect to happen?

Ideally, the migration to be compatible with MariaDB.

### Did this work before?

This broke with the migration in https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/111336

### How do we reproduce it?

1. Use a MariaDB `[database]`

```
[database]
type=mysql
host=xyz
name=grafana
user=grafana
```

2. Start the latest `grafana/grafana` Docker image


### Is the bug inside a dashboard panel?

no

### Environment (with versions)?

Grafana: 12.4.0
OS: Docker/Linux
Browser: n/a


### Grafana platform?

Docker

### Datasource(s)?

n/a

grafana/grafana#119497.body:
Metric name variable queries fail when backend mode is enabled.

This is due to changing how frames are constructed. Previously, frames were constructed in the frontend and the `name` property was set. Backend mode uses standardised data frames (abiding by the dataplane contract).

These frames do not have the `name` property set. Instead, we should determine the series name based on the `displayNameFromDS` value in the value field.

grafana/grafana#120338.body:
### What happened?

This is a somewhat nuanced issue, particularly for air-gapped environments.  If you want to disable automatically downloading the default installed plugins, you can set `plugins.preinstall_async: false`, but that also disabled any other plugins you want to install via `GF_PLUGINS_PREINSTALL_SYNC` method.  Additionally, adding the `4` default plugins to `GF_PLUGINS_PREINSTALL_SYNC` disables the installation of all plugins.

Background: there are `4` default plugins defined here that are automatically installed/updated ASYNC on startup:

https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/46a02dc12a085445ab105b72fa159248f7d1dc9d/pkg/setting/setting_plugins.go#L37-L40

Those produce some (benign) errors in air-gapped environments that can't reach the internet.  If you add those plugins to your air-gapped environment (hosted somewhere internally), you might add those to your `GF_PLUGINS_PREINSTALL_SYNC` env setting.  The logic for the plugin config removes the ASYNC plugins in favor of the SYNC plugins:

https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/46a02dc12a085445ab105b72fa159248f7d1dc9d/pkg/setting/setting_plugins.go#L168-L171

This sounds good, but once that ASYNC list is empty (you've added all `4` plugins), NO plugins are installed at all.  Very surprising indeed!

This seems to be consistent with the behavior when you set `plugins.preinstall_async: false`.  One would think the `GF_PLUGINS_PREINSTALL_SYNC` plugins would still be installed, but they are not.

I haven't fully understood the code logic, but it seems like only the `async` `PreinstallPluginsAsync` list is checked when deciding to disable the plugin service; it should probably look at both lists:

https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/46a02dc12a085445ab105b72fa159248f7d1dc9d/pkg/services/pluginsintegration/plugininstaller/service.go#L80-L82

Ultimately, I had to add the `4` default plugins to `GF_PLUGINS_PREINSTALL` (this is the async list) and then I could install additional plugins with `GF_PLUGINS_PREINSTALL_SYNC`.  This works in an air-gapped environment without any errors trying to connect to grafana.com.



### What did you expect to happen?

I should be able to disable `plugins.preinstall_async` and only install the plugins I want via `GF_PLUGINS_PREINSTALL_SYNC`, to avoid attempts to reach the internet in air-gapped environments.  Or, adding all the plugins to `GF_PLUGINS_PREINSTALL_SYNC` should not disable the plugin service.  Or, there should be a way to disable the automatic updating/installing of these default plugins but still allow other plugin installations.

### Did this work before?

Not sure

### How do we reproduce it?

1. set `plugins.preinstall_async: false` and observe no plugins are installed at startup from `GF_PLUGINS_PREINSTALL_SYNC` list
2. OR, add the `4` default plugins to `GF_PLUGINS_PREINSTALL_SYNC` and observe no plugins are installed at startup

workaround (helm chart example): add the `4` default plugins to the ASYNC env variable `GF_PLUGINS_PREINSTALL`:

```
env:
  GF_PLUGINS_PREINSTALL: "\
    grafana-exploretraces-app@1.3.3@http://internalURL/grafana/plugins/grafana-exploretraces-app-1.3.3.zip,\
    grafana-metricsdrilldown-app@1.0.34@http://internalURL/grafana/plugins/grafana-metricsdrilldown-app-1.0.34.zip,\
    grafana-pyroscope-app@1.17.0@http://internalURL/grafana/plugins/grafana-pyroscope-app-1.17.0.zip,\
    grafana-lokiexplore-app@1.0.40@http://internalURL/grafana/plugins/grafana-lokiexplore-app-1.0.40.zip"

# other plugins you might want; this is GF_PLUGINS_PREINSTALL_SYNC
plugins:
  - jdbranham-diagram-panel@1.10.4@http://internalURL/grafana/plugins/jdbranham-diagram-panel-1.10.4.zip
  - grafana-polystat-panel@2.1.16@http://internalURL/grafana/plugins/grafana-polystat-panel-2.1.16.zip
```


### Is the bug inside a dashboard panel?

No

### Environment (with versions)?

Grafana: 12.4.1
OS: Kubernetes / Helm
Browser: Safari


### Grafana platform?

Kubernetes

### Datasource(s)?

grafana/grafana#120354.body:
### What happened?

sample json : 
[render-readiness-ab-candidate.json](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25991030/render-readiness-ab-candidate.json)

Using the sample `render-readiness-ab` dashboard in this report, /render/d/... image requests take around ~12s in a reproducible setup.

If timeout=12 is applied, requests often hit the timeout boundary and return HTTP 500.
Heavier dashboards can face similar timeout-boundary failures.

I found this while building an AI-agent image-capture workflow using /render APIs.

I will open a follow-up PR for this issue; with the sample `render-readiness-ab` dashboard JSON used in this report, local test builds improved render time from ~12s to ~6-8s, reducing timeout-related failure risk.

### What did you expect to happen?

/render/d/... image rendering should complete consistently within expected latency budgets and avoid timeout-boundary HTTP 500 failures.

Render flows should have reliable readiness signaling, equivalent to other render-target routes.

### Did this work before?

Unknown (not tested on older versions)


### How do we reproduce it?

1. Run Grafana + remote renderer.
2. Import/use dashboard UID `render-readiness-ab` (3 Stat panels, `numbers + sleepEachRow` queries only, refresh=5s).
3. Send the same render request without timeout and observe latency:
   curl -u admin:admin "http://localhost:3000/render/d/render-readiness-ab?from=now-12h&to=now-11h&tz=Asia%2FSeoul&theme=dark&width=1280&height=1080"
   - official baseline: around ~13s in this setup
4. Send the same request with `timeout=12`:
   curl -u admin:admin "http://localhost:3000/render/d/render-readiness-ab?from=now-12h&to=now-11h&tz=Asia%2FSeoul&theme=dark&width=1280&height=1080&timeout=12"
   - official baseline: often HTTP 500 at timeout boundary

### Is the bug inside a dashboard panel?

No (panel-agnostic route/readiness bug).

heavy dashboards make symptoms easier to trigger.

### Environment (with versions)?

Grafana: v12.3.0 OSS (Docker image)
Renderer: grafana-image-renderer:5.0.0
OS: Docker containers (Linux)
Browser: Chrome


### Grafana platform?

Docker

### Datasource(s)?

ClickHouse 24.8

grafana/grafana#120761.body:
### What happened?

After upgrading Grafana from 12.2.1 to 12.4.1, all `alertmanager_cluster_*` metrics are now prefixed with `grafana_`, breaking existing alerting rules and dashboards that reference these metrics.

**Before (12.2.1):**
```
alertmanager_cluster_failed_peers 0
alertmanager_cluster_members 3
alertmanager_cluster_health_score 0
```

**After (12.4.1):**
```
grafana_alertmanager_cluster_failed_peers 0
grafana_alertmanager_cluster_members 3
grafana_alertmanager_cluster_health_score 0
```

This change is not documented in the release notes or changelog for 12.3 or 12.4.

### Root Cause

In `pkg/services/ngalert/metrics/ngalert.go`, the `ProvideService` function was refactored to accept a `prometheus.Registerer` via dependency injection instead of hardcoding `prometheus.DefaultRegisterer`:

**v12.2.1:**
```go
func ProvideService() *NGAlert {
    return NewNGAlert(prometheus.DefaultRegisterer)
}
```

**v12.4.1:**
```go
func ProvideService(r prometheus.Registerer) *NGAlert {
    return NewNGAlert(r)
}
```

The DI framework provides `legacyregistry.Registerer()` (from `pkg/infra/metrics/service.go:ProvideRegisterer()`). Metrics registered there are gathered by `legacyregistry.DefaultGatherer`, which is wrapped with `addPrefixWrapper` (`pkg/infra/metrics/service.go:ProvideGatherer()`). This wrapper prepends `grafana_` to all metric names that don't already start with `grafana_` or `go_`.

Previously, `prometheus.DefaultRegisterer` was used, whose gatherer (`prometheus.DefaultGatherer`) does **not** have the prefix wrapper. This caused the cluster metrics (registered by `github.com/grafana/prometheus-alertmanager/cluster` package with hardcoded names like `alertmanager_cluster_failed_peers`) to be exposed without a prefix.

### Impact

All `alertmanager_cluster_*` metrics are renamed. This breaks:
- Alerting rules monitoring HA cluster health (e.g., `alertmanager_cluster_failed_peers`)
- Dashboards referencing these metrics
- The [official Grafana meta-monitoring documentation](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/alerting/set-up/meta-monitoring/) which references the unprefixed names

### Affected Metrics

- `alertmanager_cluster_failed_peers` → `grafana_alertmanager_cluster_failed_peers`
- `alertmanager_cluster_members` → `grafana_alertmanager_cluster_members`
- `alertmanager_cluster_health_score` → `grafana_alertmanager_cluster_health_score`
- `alertmanager_cluster_alive_messages_total` → `grafana_alertmanager_cluster_alive_messages_total`
- `alertmanager_cluster_peer_info` → `grafana_alertmanager_cluster_peer_info`
- `alertmanager_cluster_pings_seconds` → `grafana_alertmanager_cluster_pings_seconds`
- `alertmanager_cluster_reconnections_total` → `grafana_alertmanager_cluster_reconnections_total`
- `alertmanager_cluster_reconnections_failed_total` → `grafana_alertmanager_cluster_reconnections_failed_total`
- `alertmanager_cluster_messages_received_total` → `grafana_alertmanager_cluster_messages_received_total`
- `alertmanager_cluster_messages_sent_total` → `grafana_alertmanager_cluster_messages_sent_total`
- (and all other `alertmanager_cluster_*` metrics)

### What's your Grafana version?

Upgraded from 12.2.1 → 12.4.1

### What's your datasource and its version?

Prometheus / Mimir

### What OS are you running Grafana on?

Linux (Kubernetes, EKS)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Deploy Grafana 12.2.1 with HA unified alerting (`ha_peers` configured)
2. Verify `alertmanager_cluster_failed_peers` metric exists at `/metrics`
3. Upgrade to 12.4.1
4. Observe metric is now `grafana_alertmanager_cluster_failed_peers`

### Expected behavior

Either:
1. The metric names should remain unchanged (no `grafana_` prefix) for backwards compatibility, or
2. This should be documented as a breaking change in the release notes with migration guidance

### Relevant environment info

grafana/grafana#121213.body:
The `panelStyleActions` feature (copy/paste panel styles via the panel header menu) was built as a PoC hardcoded to timeseries panels only. This issue tracks making the architecture extensible so more panel types can participate without code changes to the core copy/paste logic.                                                                             

#### Current behavior:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
  - The "Styles" submenu in the panel header menu only appears for timeseries panels                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
  - Copying and pasting styles is blocked for all other panel types      
  - 3 places in the code hardcode timeseries:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
    - [PanelMenuBehavior.tsx](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/2be34267b287becff29ea6744883d0e1c4fd1729/public/app/features/dashboard-scene/scene/PanelMenuBehavior.tsx) - menu visibility check                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
    - [DashboardScene.copyPanelStyles](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/d1a5146d66bdb68c35e7bcb2757d8ddfad8d91b7/public/app/features/dashboard-scene/scene/DashboardScene.tsx#L728) - early return guard                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
    - [DashboardScene.hasPanelStylesToPaste](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/d1a5146d66bdb68c35e7bcb2757d8ddfad8d91b7/public/app/features/dashboard-scene/scene/DashboardScene.tsx#L742) call           


### Scope                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 
                                                                         
Introduce a central panel style config registry (maybe `panelStyleConfigs.ts`) that maps pluginId -> PanelStyleConfig. The registry declares which fieldConfig keys count as "style" properties for each panel type.                                                                                                                                                                      
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
The hardcoded timeseries checks in `DashboardScene` and `PanelMenuBehavior` should be replaced with a `getPanelStyleConfig(pluginId)` lookup.

Note - timeseries, trend and candlestick have the same `GraphFieldConfig` so it might be easy to start with those.

Expand the functionality to more panels (could be top 5 most used panels or more). 
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
### Out of scope                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
  - Paste across different panel types                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    
  - External plugins

grafana/grafana#121317.body:
### Background ...

The current coverage comparison threshold of `< 0.00%` may be too strict when comparing the code coverage from an incoming PR's branch to that of the main branch.

Additionally, coverage from the most recent main branch is compared with the PR feature branch, even when the feature branch is based on an older version of main.

### Why?

When code coverage tools round test coverage figures with even a minor discrepancy a false failure is caused. These false failures pollute the signal:noise ratio of this test tooling.

### Solution!

1.  Explore if we can make the test coverage more accurate
    - If not, refactor the comparison script to use an epsilon value of `< 0.02%` for comparisons instead
3. Merge latest main into the feature branch before calculating code coverage (OK to fail on merge conflicts)
grafana/grafana#121317.title: CI: Test coverage comparisons are sometimes inaccurate

grafana/logs-drilldown#1704.body:
In an effort to reduce costs by reducing cardinality in our metrics and other instrumentation, and to facilitate migration off of the old feature flag system,[Dave Henderson](mailto:dave.henderson@grafana.com) put together [a list of all the feature toggles that are older than one year, for each squad](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1Ww2ibkZ9ZQOx58QTQiLue5SqjH8jnHfhIGdgE1w7Vng/edit?gid=0#gid=0&fvid=856216879).  He’s asked that we take action on these feature flags:

- If the features are not going to progress beyond experiments, let's consider deleting them
- If they are going to progress, let's prioritize that
- If they should really just be settings (not feature flags), let's fix that

For Logs Drilldown, there are several:  

- [x] [lokiExperimentalStreaming](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/116205)
- [x] logsExploreTableDefaultVisualization,
- [x] [exploreLogsShardSplitting](https://github.com/grafana/logs-drilldown/pull/1766) (keep and migrate to the new feature flag system)
- [x] [exploreLogsLimitedTimeRange](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/116177)
- [x] exploreLogsAggregatedMetrics, (Loki squad won't have time to promote aggregated metrics API to GA by deadline so we need to convert this to the new feature flag format. Trevor won't let Galen delete it.)
- [ ] lokiShardSplitting (keep and migrate to the new feature flag system. reason: we need a scape hatch to disable it if needed)
- [x] logsContextDatasourceUi already FeatureStageGeneralAvailability (remove)
- [ ] [lokiLogsDataplane](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/116205) (oss-big-tent) meet with them turn on everywhere, etc data frame contact spec, time and line to timestamp and body. Only affects logs not metrics.
- [ ] [lokiQueryLimitsContext](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/120458) (less then 1 year old, but needs to be long lived toggle, keep within the Drilldown team because it's related with a Logs Drilldown feature)
- [ ] logsExploreTableVisualisation: enabled by default for a long time, remove
- [ ] logsPanelControls: enabled by default for a long time. Make the controlled logs panels default.

The task is to make a decision about them that allows us to remove the feature toggle, then implement the decision.  So, if the decision is that the functionality controlled by this feature toggle is no longer needed, the implementation will be to remove the toggle and the functionality.  Alternatively, the decision could be to promote the functionality to be unconditionally available and remove the toggle.  Or, as a third alternative, the toggle needs to be turned into a setting.  

Once the decision has been made and implemented, please also go back to the spreadsheet and update the status for the feature toggle.

# Docs and Example in Metric

- [Metrics](https://github.com/grafana/metrics-drilldown/pull/886), [deployment tools](https://github.com/grafana/deployment_tools/tree/master/ksonnet/environments/hosted-grafana/waves/feature-toggles/goff/drilldown)
- [Docs](https://enghub.grafana-ops.net/docs/default/Component/grafana-backend-services-docs/openfeature/feature-flags-with-open-feature), how does this work in core?

- Try converting one to the GOFF shard splitting
- Start the easy stuff now and follow up in Q1
- App platform is using the old or new toggles? 😮‍💨 
- There are more flags: logsContext?

grafana/nanogit#242.body:
### Problem

`CompareCommits` currently identifies file changes by comparing two flat trees by path. When a file is renamed (e.g., `git mv foo.txt bar.txt`), the result is a `Deleted` + `Added` pair instead of a single `Renamed` entry. Similarly, copied files are never detected — they just appear as `Added`.

This is visible in the existing integration tests (`tests/commit_integration_test.go:209-222`), where a `git mv test.txt renamed.txt` produces:
- `renamed.txt` → `Added`
- `test.txt` → `Deleted`

instead of the expected:
- `renamed.txt` → `Renamed` (from `test.txt`)

### What needs to change

#### 1. Add new status constants in `protocol/delta.go`

Add `FileStatusRenamed` (`"R"`) and `FileStatusCopied` (`"C"`) to the existing `FileStatus` constants alongside `M`, `A`, `D`, `T`.

#### 2. Add `OldPath` field to `CommitFile` in `commit.go`

The `CommitFile` struct needs an `OldPath string` field to record the original path for renamed/copied files. The existing `Path` field would hold the new path, and `OldPath` would hold the source path.

#### 3. Add `CompareCommitsOption` with `WithRenameDetection()`

Since enabling rename/copy detection changes the output of `CompareCommits` (replacing `Added`+`Deleted` pairs with `Renamed` entries), this is a **breaking change** for existing consumers.

To preserve backwards compatibility, follow the existing functional options pattern (see `WriterOption` in `writer_options.go`):
- Add a `CompareCommitsOption` type and a `WithRenameDetection()` option.
- Rename/copy detection is **off by default** — existing callers see no behavior change.
- Users opt in: `client.CompareCommits(ctx, base, head, nanogit.WithRenameDetection())`.

This requires updating the `Client` interface signature from:
```go
CompareCommits(ctx context.Context, baseCommit, headCommit hash.Hash) ([]CommitFile, error)
```
to:
```go
CompareCommits(ctx context.Context, baseCommit, headCommit hash.Hash, opts ...CompareCommitsOption) ([]CommitFile, error)
```

This is a minor interface breaking change (implementors need to add the parameter), but callers without options continue to compile unchanged.

#### 4. Implement rename/copy detection in `compareTrees()` (`commit.go:190`)

When rename detection is enabled, add a post-processing step after the current added/deleted/modified logic:

- **Rename detection**: If a deleted file's hash matches an added file's hash (identical content), emit a single `Renamed` entry with `OldPath` set to the deleted path, removing both the `Added` and `Deleted` entries.
- **Copy detection**: If an added file's hash matches a file that still exists in the base tree (not deleted), replace the `Added` entry with a `Copied` entry with `OldPath` set to the source path.
- **Similarity threshold (optional/future)**: Git's native rename detection uses a configurable similarity threshold (default 50%) to detect renames even when content was modified. An initial implementation could require exact hash matches only, with similarity-based detection as a follow-up.

#### 5. Update and add integration tests (`tests/commit_integration_test.go`)

The existing "should compare modified and renamed commits" test cases (lines 209-237) currently assert `Added` + `Deleted` for renames — these should remain as-is to validate default (no detection) behavior. New test cases should be added with `WithRenameDetection()` for:
- Pure rename (no content change)
- Rename with content modification (if similarity detection is implemented)
- File copy detection
- Edge cases: multiple renames, rename chains, rename + modify in same commit

#### 6. Regenerate mocks

Run `make generate` to update the `FakeClient` mock in `mocks/client.go` after the interface change.

### References

- Git diff raw output format (status letters M, A, D, R, C, T, etc.): https://git-scm.com/docs/git-diff#_raw_output_format
- Git status short format: https://git-scm.com/docs/git-status#_short_format
- Current limitation noted in `protocol/delta.go:15`
grafana/nanogit#242.title: Add support for Renamed (R) and Copied (C) file status codes
